title: ILAC Specifying Accreditation as part of COVID-19 Testing
excerpt: International Laboratories Accreditation Cooperation (ILAC) published a new brochure “Specifying Accreditation as part of COVID-19 Testing”
content:
## ILAC Specifying Accreditation as part of COVID-19 Testing

Many regulators are already using accreditation effectively to support their regulatory and policy objectives in areas of national interest including health.

International Laboratories Accreditation Cooperation (ILAC) published a new brochure “Specifying Accreditation as part of COVID-19 Testing”, for those not previously involved in this process, the lab shall be accredited in accordance with medical labs accreditation standard requirements of ISO 15189, and the testing lab’s scope of accreditation shall include SARS-CoV-2-testing.

Laboratories involved in this testing may be using commercially produced test kits or fully validated in-house test procedures. In some economies, there may be government/regulatory requirements in relation to the testing protocols and/or the need for accreditation.

title: Welcome on board W.E.S
content:
## Welcome on board W.E.S

AAA would like to happily welcome a new member with us which is the W.E.S ( worldwide encouragement society ) , as they are the first organizational member representing Kenya in the forum of The American Accreditation association .

We are excited to have you on abroad .

AAA is sure that together we can take the organization to new heights. Welcome Aboard!

title: AAA ASTM E2659 training implementation
content:
## AAA ASTM E2659 training implementation

We are pleased to inform you that during the 14th and the 15th of January 2021, AAA accreditation has arranged an online training course elucidated for the assessors of the standard ASTM E2659 covering all information related to the accreditation certificate issuers/training providers.

The training has been successfully conducted online with the presence of 8 participants from the USA and other international countries.

title: IAF Statement on COVID-19
content:
## IAF Statement on COVID-19

IAF continues to monitor advice to assess the developments and risks following the outbreak of coronavirus disease (COVID-19).

The situation has led to safety concerns and travel restrictions in relevant economies that are likely to affect accredited conformity assessment activities.

Following the guidance set out in ID3:2011 (IAF Informative Document For Management of Extraordinary Events or Circumstances Affecting ABs, CABs and Certified Organizations), relevant Accreditation Bodies and Certification Bodies have introduced or are introducing alternative arrangements to conduct assessments. This may involve e.g. making arrangements to conduct assessments remotely or rescheduling activities if necessary.

Both ABs and CBs can find the requirements on how to use information and communication technologies to support and maintain the integrity of the audit/assessment process in IAF MD 4 (Mandatory Document for the Use of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) for Auditing/Assessment Purposes).

These measures have been/are being put in place to ensure the health and safety of Accreditation Body and Certification Body staff, as well as to maintain robust accredited certification and ensure certified organizations are served in the best way possible.

title: November is World Quality Month
content:
## November is World Quality Month

## Join our celebration

**AAA announces the celebration of the** **World Quality Month** **in November 2020**

Celebrating the World Quality Month in November is an opportunity to review how we are doing, share our accomplishments and continue growing. Below are some ideas that can help you get the best out of this month.

- Organize brainstorming sessions with your teams to capture ideas and improvement opportunities.

- Train your team on the application of the classic quality tools like the fishbone diagram, the PDSA cycle or the 5S and also explore other tools like Design thinking and agile practices.

- Arrange a coffee learning event about quality at your organization.

- Arrange for a quiz to your staff related to quality issues and ideas for improvement.

title: IASA Academy gained AAA Full Membership
content:
## IASA Academy gained AAA Full Membership

The Turkish institution called IASA Academy – that is specialized in delivering academic and training programs and courses – gained the AAA Full Membership after achieving the requirements of getting such membership.
Also, the membership certificate can be verified at any time on our website in the American Directory for Accredited Organizations – ADAO, via the following link:

[IASA Membership](https://lnkd.in/daGJ_CA).

AAA appreciates the efforts done by IASA Academy Quality teamwork and advisers to achieve the requirements of getting the membership under the standard of AAA regarding to such subject.
Also, All the organizations that have the full membership of AAA as training & education providers or as personnel certification bodies can add their certified trainees to the American Directory of Competent Personnel – ADCP – and get specially issued certificates by the accreditation body (Against a sum of money to be paid for each certificate), and they can use the membership mark on their websites and all their marketing tools and issued certificates.

title: World Day for Safety and Health at Work
excerpt: Recognizing the great challenge that governments, employers, workers and whole societies are facing worldwide …
content:
## World Day for Safety and Health at Work

Stop the Pandemic: Safety and health at work can save lives

#WorldDayforSafetyandHealthatWork2020

Recognizing the great challenge that governments, employers, workers and whole societies are facing worldwide to combat the COVID-19 pandemic, the World Day for Safety and Health at Work will focus on addressing the outbreak of infectious diseases at work, in particular, on the COVID-19 pandemic.

Concern is growing over the continuing rise in COVID-19 infections in some parts of the world and the ability to sustain declining rates in others. Governments, employers, workers and their organizations face enormous challenges as they try to combat the COVID-19 pandemic and protect safety and health at work. Beyond the immediate crisis, there are also concerns about resuming activity in a manner that sustains progress made in suppressing transmission.

The World Day for Safety and Health at Work will aim to stimulate national tripartite dialogue on safety and health at work. The ILO is using this day to raise awareness on the adoption of safe practices in workplaces and the role that occupational safety and health (OSH) services play. It will also focus on the medium to long-term, including recovery and future preparedness, in particular, integrating measures into OSH management systems and policies at the national and enterprise levels.

Read more on the United Nations website via the following link:

[https://www.un.org/en/observances/work-safety-day](https://www.un.org/en/observances/work-safety-day)

Read more on the International Labor Organization – ILO – via the following link:

[https://www.ilo.org/global/topics/safety-and-health-at-work/events-training/events-meetings/world-day-safety-health-at-work/WCMS_739669/lang–en/index.htm](https://www.ilo.org/global/topics/safety-and-health-at-work/events-training/events-meetings/world-day-safety-health-at-work/WCMS_739669/lang--en/index.htm)
